description: Manifest for a public repo that receives output from a private Rappter engine. The engine pulls this manifest, reads the chain state, computes the next frame, and pushes it back. The engine IP stays private. The output is static and public.

frames.description: Environmental frames. One per sol. Static JSON. The engine writes these.

chain.description: Hash-linked verification chain. The engine's frame becomes a vote in the chain.

pull.1_read_manifest: GET data/frames/manifest.json → know what frames exist
pull.2_read_latest: GET data/frames/latest.json → know the chain head
pull.4_read_last_frame: GET data/frames/sol-NNNN.json → read prev frame for echo continuity

compute.5_generate_frame: Engine runs its private algorithms to produce the next frame
compute.6_hash_frame: SHA-256 of frame content (before hash insertion) for anti-tamper
compute.7_link_chain: prevHash = hash of previous frame → extends the chain
compute.8_sign_vote: Engine signs the frame with its node ID → attribution

push.10_update_manifest: PUT data/frames/manifest.json → add frame to index
push.11_update_latest: PUT data/frames/latest.json → update chain head pointer
push.12_commit: git commit + push → the frame is now public and immutable

engine_protocol.description: Pull → Compute → Push. The compute step is private. The pull and push are public. This is how the engine keeps its IP while driving the public chain.

multi_engine_voting.description: Multiple engines can target the same repo. Each engine reads the latest frame, computes its proposed next frame, and submits via PR or direct push. If engines disagree, the repo owner (or a GitHub Action) picks the winning frame. This is consensus without a blockchain protocol — git branch resolution IS the consensus mechanism.
multi_engine_voting.conflict_resolution: First valid push wins (fast-forward). Conflicting pushes require PR review. Chain nodes verify the winning frame.
